paragraphs.body: It’s me Zlata, what happened to us? I thought we were best friends forever where we would always have our backs for each other.
Do you not remember how you treated me back in high school? How you betrayed me.
… No, I do and I’m really sorry about that, we were young back then and they were treating me nicely at first which became betrayal to me as well.

I don’t see that as an excuse for treating me like that, I even told you that you should stop hanging out with them, but you wouldn’t even listen to me and was being selfish. Because of that you got into bad habits that had me worried and you even FORCED me to try things out when I specifically said no. ….

annotations.text: I don’t see that as an excuse for treating me like that, I even told you that you should stop hanging out with them, but you wouldn’t even listen to me and was being selfish. Because of that you got into bad habits that had me worried and you even FORCED me to try things out when I specifically said no
annotations.comment: I don't want to ask you to share anything that you're not comfortable sharing, but, in order for this piece to gain strength, moments like this could really use some clarification, some concrete and specific details.

paragraphs.body: I always knew they were bad for you, yet you kept hanging out with them to look cool and get a bunch attention from a lot of people because of that. I’m sorry about everything… Can we possibly start our friendship over? I won’t do it ever again. I don’t know if I could restart our friendship, especially when you were being two faced with me and my other friends. I had to find out from your toxic friend that you were talking shit about me and my friends about how we are “cringe” and how we don’t really fit your standards. I apologize about that, but I didn’t mean it, I only said it to fit in with them because they always talk behind other people’s back… That doesn’t give you an excuse on talking behind my back, whether you meant it or not. Because of that you lost your true friends and suffered the rest of your high school life with them.

I know I realized, and I regret everything I did. At the end I found out that they were talking behind my back and started to hang out without me because I didn’t match their standards and even got replaced by a different girl. You should’ve known better and listened to me when I told you that they were a bad influence.

Is there really no way of making up our friendship? I realize that you were my true true friend. I promise that this will never happen again. If you really do promise me then I’ll give you a chance, but I don’t want to see anything like this again.
Mia why aren’t you texting anymore? Did something happen?
Are you really asking. Do you not know what happened in New York.
I’m not quite sure what… could you explain?
You once again ditched me for your other friend that came on this trip.

annotations.text: You once again ditched me for your other friend that came on this trip.
annotations.comment: Here is a moment where your reader will get a much clearer sense of the pain your friend has caused you--we know where you are in the world and that your friend has abandoned you.

paragraphs.body: Oh, I didn’t even realize that happened. I thought you were okay with everything. I acted like everything was okay because I didn’t want you friend to feel bad about anything. This trip was supposed to be our trip, but it became you and your other friend’s trip that you invited last minute. I’m sorry I didn’t mean for things to happen like this…

It’s too late for any of that, you didn’t really consider my feelings throughout the trip and ask if it was okay for your friend to join us when we had our own plans. The plans I have made for us was pretty much ruined because of that. I thought I did ask, maybe because you just looked okay with everything, I thought it was okay.

Sure, but it would’ve been nice to ask me about my feelings. You would often just leave me alone at the hotel and go hang out with her and just betray me like that. It’s as if I was just invisible to you. It’s nice that you made a new friend but if you can just forget about me and replace me like that then don’t ask to do things with me please. …I’m sorry I have nothing to say about that…

I have given you a chance and you have proved me wrong. I thought you actually changed, but all you do is just leave me on the side and come back to me when your other friends have no business with you. I’m just a friend you keep as a safe option to come back to whenever you want and that’s not the friend I made back in high school. I don’t think I can deal with this anymore, go find a real friend you can be loyal to.

annotations.comment: Hello, Clearly, the central focusing idea of this piece is the relationship between you and an old friend and the eventual feelings of betrayal that developed between the two of you. You’re doing a great job here of experimenting with form and developing a clear communication between the two of you through your own experiences and from your own perspective. A great strength of this piece is how intensely you focus on a few particular moments in your relationship and develop those moments to form a single narrative thread. The words that stood out the most to me were the variations of the word friend. In reading this piece, I felt the tenuous nature of friendship, because the word has so many different meanings throughout the writing. It made me think of some friendships I’ve had where I’ve been really close to someone and, at some point, one of us hurt the other one. Sometimes we endure those pains and work through them to rebuild the friendship. Other times, it’s just healthier to let it go and end the relationship, but I often still refer to those people as an old friend or an ex friend, even though I don’t consider them a friend at all anymore. I’m really feeling those experiences afresh in reading your writing. What do we call a friend who is no longer a friend? You’re dealing with a tiny and impossible question to answer here. The audience of this piece is clearly your old friend and, in a lot of ways, yourself. And that’s a great place to start, but I could see this developing into a much more far reaching and culturally significant piece. I believe if you shared more specific details about the betrayals, more readers would be able to connect and empathize with your experiences. You’re doing a great job here of sharing very personal experiences, but it is hard for a reader to intuit what actually happened. We get our strongest understanding of the situation in New York, but even that could use some sensuous details. Meanwhile, the current purpose of this piece seems to be to litigate the relationship between your past self and your former friend, but exploring your own place within this toxic relationship could have great value to others your age in terms of dealing with their own relationships. It’s the people who mean the most to us who can cause us the greatest harm, but I’m curious at what point one ends the conversation permanently. I wonder how it affects opening up to new friendships in the future. While I would definitely love to gain a deeper understanding of this relationship, one thing I did learn from this is how important your friend was/is to you. If they were not important, they would not be living rent free in your brain to this day. This could be another idea that you develop in this writing: how do we navigate celebrating the good times we’ve had with someone after a relationship has soured and ended? Thanks for This,
